## TL;DR
Mandatory TL;DR does not lose evidence: the full Argument is still written to file as before; TL;DR is just an index layer.
The moderator reads the full text on demand and won't misjudge convergence.
[stance: hold]

## Argument
The challenge presupposes the wrong model — "TL;DR replaces Argument". The actual design has both layers coexisting:
the full Argument (150–300 words, with code references and edge cases) is always written to `$DEBATE_DIR/{role}-rN.txt`.
TL;DR is just the sed-extracted index; at checkpoint time the moderator still Reads the full Argument on demand.
